headline: US Banks Sound Alarm On Lower-Income Struggles Before Election
summary: Financial giants warn of lower-income woes pre-election, amid depleted stimulus impact and inflation fears.

headline: Tesla Q2 EPS Estimate Bumped Up, Rivian's Cold Shoulder To Union, Lucid's 70% Sales Jump And More: Biggest EV Stories Of The Week
summary: EV fundamentals continue to bounce around the bottom with companies awaiting an inflection in demand and a respite from price cuts.

headline: Benzinga Bulls And Bears: Apple, Nvidia, Palantir, MicroStrategy And Shiba Inu's Ambitious Trillion-Dollar Vision
summary:
Benzinga examined the prospects for many investors’ favorite stocks over the last week — here’s a look at some of our top stories.



The Dow led the market with a 1.6% weekly gain, while a sharp sell-off in tech stocks limited the Nasdaq’s increase to 0.2%, and the S&P 500 rose by 0.9%.

headline: Analyst Ups Apple's Price Target To $256, Says New AI Features Will Encourage Users To Upgrade To Newer iPhones: 'People Were Looking For A Real Reason...'
summary: Bank of America Securities analyst Wamsi Mohan has increased his price target for Apple from $230 to $256, citing the potential for an upgrade cycle driven by new AI features.

headline: OpenAI's Q* Gets New Name, Project Strawberry: Report Says It Can Navigate Internet Autonomously With 'Deep Research' And Significantly Better Reasoning Capabilities
summary: OpenAI, the creator of ChatGPT, is reportedly working on a novel artificial intelligence project, codenamed "Strawberry."

headline: As Tesla CEO Elon Musk Wants To 'Obliterate' Short Sellers, Expert Says They Are Important 'More Than Ever' To Keep Frauds Out Of The Market
summary: Muddy Waters founder Carson Block believes short sellers are crucial for curbing market fraud, but the current speculative frenzy makes their job difficult.

headline: Meta Removes 'Heightened Penalties' On Donald Trump's Accounts A Day After Ex-President Threatens To Put Mark Zuckerberg In Jail
summary: Meta has lifted the additional restrictions previously imposed on former U.S. President Donald Trump's accounts, allowing him to use Facebook and Instagram under standard community guidelines as he is expected to be the Republican presidential nominee.
